mnemonics: M.I.S.S.I.N.G. - My Investigation Shows Signals, Investigate, Notify, and Guide conclusions
keyPoints: First, identify patterns of missingness (MCAR, MAR, MNAR) to choose the right strategy.
Simple imputation (mean/median/mode) is quick but can distort data distributions and variance.
Advanced methods like Multiple Imputation create several plausible values for the missing data, accounting for uncertainty.
commonMistakes: Deleting missing data (listwise deletion) without checking the pattern, leading to biased results.
Imputing data without considering the mechanism (why it's missing), e.g., using mean for MNAR data.
Forgetting that imputed values are estimates, not real observations, and treating them with the same certainty.
